name: Buell Neidlinger
description: American jazz musician; cellist and double bassist (1936–2018)
abstract: Buell Neidlinger (March 2, 1936 – March 16, 2018) was an American cellist and double bassist. He worked with a variety of pop and jazz performers, prominently with iconoclastic pianist Cecil Taylor in the 1950s and '60s.

works.t: In Re Con Moto et al.
Movements for Flute, Double Bass, Vibraphone and Percussion
Piano Quintet in A Major D. 667, "Die Forelle"

about_quotes.speaker: Roswell Rudd
about_quotes.evidence: There was a bass player named Buell Neidlinger... he invited me to play with them... do gigs with them
about_quotes.doc_title: Roswell Rudd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: But the people who were my friends – Buell Neidlinger, Charlie Haden, and all those guys – were all people who were thought of as a part of the avant garde.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: Buell Neidlinger, Bill Nyad, a bassist that I knew; a white dude, that had been working mostly with Cecil Skeets Taylor.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
